courses.entry_requirements: Entry level 1 (72 points): An Australian bachelors degree, or an equivalent qualification, with at least a credit (60%) average, or an equivalent Grade Point Average, plus two years relevant work experience. Applicants must provide a detailed curriculum vitae. Entry level 2 (48 points): A Monash University Graduate Certificate of Human Resource Management with at least a credit (60%) average. English Requirements: Applicants must meet the English language requirements.

core_subjects_modules.value: Part A. Advanced preparatory studies: Diversity and inclusion
Ethics and legal issues in human resource management
Sustainability and performance in human resource management
Part B. Core studies: Engagement and talent management
Mental health and wellbeing in the workplace
Part C (i) Application studies: Professional development
Part C (ii) Application studies (complete one or both): Industry challenge
Electives: You must complete either two or three elective units, depending on whether one or both industry project units listed above were chosen

courses.outcomes: HR Advisor: $95,000 typical salary. Handles recruitment, staff issues, performance, and training. HR Consultant: $103,000 typical salary. Provides advice to management, coordinates workplace health and safety, and leads wellbeing initiatives. Organisational Change Manager: $140,000 typical salary. Implements changes to processes, systems, and technology; conducts performance assessments. Human Resources Manager: $130,000 typical salary. Oversees recruitment, employee relations, and organizational development.

courses.course_name: Master of Human Resource Management (with specialisations)
courses.course_description: Shift your thinking and add value to your organisation with the Master of Human Resource Management (with specialisations) from Charles Sturt University. If you're an HR practitioner wishing to accelerate your career in a highly competitive marketplace, this online degree will allow you to sharpen your strategic, analytical and practice-based skills. This course provides the breadth and depth of HR knowledge that contemporary professionals require, presenting the latest in HR management best practice to deliver benefits for individuals and organisations. Gain skills to progress your career and become an asset to your workplace as you apply your advanced knowledge and capabilities.
courses.tuition_fees: Domestic online fee paying postgraduate: Full-time - $33,680.00 pa; Part-time - $16,840.00 pa. International fees: TBA (The page notes Fees are indicative only, based on full-time study).
courses.fee_help_eligibility: Eligible students have the option to defer their fees through FEE-HELP. Payment options include getting help to cover the cost of tuition fees via a FEE-HELP loan through the Australian Government (if eligible). For Commonwealth supported places, eligible students may defer their student contribution via a HECS-HELP loan.
courses.course_duration: Minimum time - 1.5 year(s); Maximum time - 3 year(s)
courses.entry_requirements: An undergraduate degree from a recognised Australian tertiary institution (or equivalent). Applicants without a tertiary qualification may be admitted to the Graduate Certificate in Human Resource Management on the basis of prior post secondary study, professional attainment and/or work experience. Standard English Language Proficiency (ELP) requirements apply.
courses.recognition_of_prior_learning: If you've completed relevant tertiary study, or work experience related to your course, you could receive credit towards your degree. Which would mean completing your qualification quicker.

core_subjects_modules.value: INR517: Legal Frameworks of Employment Relations
HRM514: Cross Cultural and International HRM
HRM563: Organisational Development and Workforce Capabilities

courses.outcomes: Our graduates have moved into positions as diverse as human resource director, industrial relations manager, change manager, workforce consultant and organisational development manager. Your degree also prepares you to rise to the next level of management by helping you develop knowledge and skills to identify and solve complex problems, pursue a strategic approach to HR management practice, and implement effective solutions to achieve business goals. The 2025/26 Good Universities Guide ranks the university 5-stars for business postgraduate salaries with a median graduate salary of $127,100.

courses.course_description: Deakin's Master of Human Resource Management will help you develop up-to-date skills to implement contemporary HR strategies that are highly impactful at both the individual and organisational level. Whether you're working directly in human resource management (HRM) or in general management with responsibilities in human resources development and management, extend your skills and knowledge with this formal qualification. Accredited by the Australian Human Resources Institute, this specialised course is designed to provide enhanced qualifications for graduates in business, government and industry. Undertake units that are highly relevant to industry, including employment relations, human resource strategy, leadership and management development.
courses.tuition_fees: Domestic: $34,200 for 1 yr full-time - Full-fee paying place. The 'Estimated tuition fee' is provided as a guide only and represents the typical first-year tuition fees for students enrolled in this course.
courses.fee_help_eligibility: You may be eligible for a HELP loan – including FEE-HELP or HECS-HELP – depending on your course, type of fee place, and your citizenship or residency status.
courses.course_duration: 1.5 years. Can be reduced (fast tracked) to 1 year duration based on previous qualifications and professional experience.
courses.entry_requirements: To be considered for admission you will need to meet at least one of the following criteria: Completion of a bachelor degree or higher in any discipline and at least two years' relevant work experience (or part-time equivalent); OR completion of a graduate certificate or higher in a related discipline and at least two years' relevant work experience (or part-time equivalent). Relevant work experience includes at least two years in a managerial role with people management responsibilities or in a human resource professional role. English language proficiency: IELTS overall score of 6.5 (no band score less than 6.0) or equivalent.
courses.recognition_of_prior_learning: The University aims to provide students with as much credit as possible for approved prior study or informal learning. Students are required to complete a minimum of one-third of the course at Deakin (4 credit points). Recognition of prior learning may be granted to students who have completed appropriate postgraduate studies in a related discipline. Recognition of prior learning cannot be granted on the basis of relevant work experience alone as this is a requirement of entry. Applicants who have completed the AHRI Practising Certification Program (2016-current) may be eligible for up to 4 credit points of RPL.

core_subjects_modules.value: DAI001 Academic Integrity and Respect at Deakin (0-credit-point compulsory unit)
MMH711 The New World of Work (Capstone)

courses.outcomes: Graduates are equipped for advanced and strategic roles including: human resource manager, People and culture director, human resource business partner, talent acquisition manager, learning and development manager, diversity and inclusion manager, employee relations specialist, human resource data analyst, and organisational development consultant.

courses.course_description: Gain in-demand, contemporary marketing knowledge and develop the sharp communication and critical thinking skills that employers are looking for. Experience hands-on practical learning across martech, digital and social media marketing, marketing analytics and sustainability for business, allowing you to develop contemporary marketing knowledge that will set you apart in the industry. Learn how to develop, implement and evaluate effective marketing strategies and put your skills into practice with industry relevant learning opportunities. Throughout your degree, you will gain practical experience through case studies and client-based projects and graduate with the confidence and skills to thrive.
courses.tuition_fees: Domestic: Estimated tuition fee - full-fee paying place: $34,200 for 1 yr full-time.
courses.fee_help_eligibility: You may be eligible for a HELP loan – including FEE-HELP or HECS-HELP – depending on your course, type of fee place, and your citizenship or residency status.
courses.course_duration: 2 years, 1.5 years, or 1 year duration depending on previous qualifications and professional experience
courses.entry_requirements: 1 year (8 credit points): Bachelor honours (AQF8) degree in a related discipline; bachelor degree in a related discipline, plus two years relevant work experience; graduate certificate or graduate diploma in a related discipline; or evidence of academic capability judged to be equivalent. 1.5 years (12 credit points): Bachelor degree or higher in a related discipline; bachelor degree in any discipline, plus two years relevant work experience; or evidence of academic capability judged to be equivalent. 2 years (16 credit points): Bachelor degree or other qualification at a higher AQF level in any discipline; or evidence of academic capability judged to be equivalent. English language proficiency: IELTS overall score of 6.5 (with no band score less than 6.0) or equivalent; or a bachelor degree from a recognised English-speaking country.
courses.recognition_of_prior_learning: The University aims to provide students with as much credit as possible for approved prior study or informal learning. You can refer to the recognition of prior learning (RPL) system which outlines the credit that may be granted towards a Deakin University degree and how to apply for credit.

core_subjects_modules.value: DAI001 Academic Integrity and Respect at Deakin (0-credit-point compulsory unit)
MIS770 Foundation Skills in Data Analysis
MMK737 Digital and Social Media Marketing
Students also complete 1 credit point from: MMK739 Strategic Brand Management or MMK751 Services Marketing

courses.outcomes: Graduates will be qualified to work across traditional marketing, non-traditional marketing, or agency work. Potential roles include: brand manager, content developer and manager, digital marketing manager, director of marketing, marketing manager, marketing officer, market research manager, marketing specialist, social media manager, and marketing analyst.
